title: Chicken Shawarma Bowls with Lemon Rice
description: Air-fried shawarma-spiced chicken breast over fluffy lemon rice with quick-pickled green cabbage and a creamy Greek yogurt feta sauce. High-protein weeknight bowl with serious flavour.

inspiredBy.name: Trader Joe's Chicken Shawarma with Lemon Rice

ingredients: 680g / 24 oz boneless skinless chicken breast (6 oz per serving), diced into 1-inch cubes
1 tbsp Better Than Bouillon chicken base
1 tbsp extra virgin olive oil (for sauce)
1 tsp dried dill (or 3 tbsp fresh dill)
instructions: Marinate the chicken: Combine 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp coriander, 1 tsp smoked paprika, ½ tsp turmeric, ¼ tsp cinnamon, 3 cloves minced garlic, 1 tbsp olive oil, 2 tbsp lemon juice, and ½ tsp salt in a bowl. Dice 680g / 24 oz chicken breast into 1-inch cubes and coat thoroughly. Marinate at least 20 minutes (or up to overnight in the fridge).

Start the lemon rice: Rinse 1½ cups white rice thoroughly in a colander. Add to a medium pot with 1 tsp onion powder, 1 tsp garlic powder, ½ tsp kosher salt, 1 tbsp Better Than Bouillon, and 2¼ cups water. Stir well and bring to a simmer. Cover and cook on very low heat for 18 minutes. Remove from heat, stir in 2 tsp lemon juice, and fluff with a fork.

Make the quick-pickled cabbage slaw: Toss 2 cups shredded green cabbage with ¼ cup white vinegar, ¼ cup water, 1 tbsp canola oil, ½ tsp salt, and ½ tsp maple syrup. Toss well and set aside — it pickles as it sits.

Make the yogurt feta sauce: Stir together ½ cup 0% plain Greek yogurt, ¼ cup crumbled feta, 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 clove grated garlic, and 1 tsp dried dill (or 3 tbsp fresh). Taste and adjust seasoning. Refrigerate until serving.

Air fry the chicken: Preheat air fryer to 390°F. Add diced chicken in a single layer and cook for 10–12 minutes, shaking the basket at the 6-minute mark, until internal temp reaches 165°F and edges are browned. Do not overcrowd — cook in batches if needed.
Assemble: Divide lemon rice evenly across 4 bowls. Top each with diced chicken, a scoop of pickled cabbage slaw, and a generous dollop of yogurt feta sauce.
notes: Marinade time matters — even 20 minutes makes a difference; overnight is better. The yogurt feta sauce splits when heated, so keep it cold and add at the last second. Slaw gets better the longer it sits, so making it first is ideal. For a lower-carb version, sub half the rice with cauliflower rice — saves roughly 90 calories and 20g carbs per serving.
